commonMistakes: Memorizing syntax without predicting the underlying value or execution order.
Mutating shared data when the task expects a new value or explicit ownership.
Treating browser APIs and language features as the same layer.
Choosing an abstraction from habit rather than required operations.

contentAuthority.canonical: New JS2Next Release 1 curriculum and independently authored content.
contentAuthority.courseTranscripts: Coverage and sequencing research only; never learner-facing authority.
contentAuthority.legacyProjectContent: Supplementary internal reference only; excluded from runtime unless explicitly re-authored and approved.

learningObjectives.text: Apply feature decomposition using a new example and justify the result.
Explain project architecture planning using a new example and justify the result.
Trace and explain data and ui flow using a new example and justify the result.

mentalModel: Draw a decision map: **Feature decomposition** → **Project architecture planning** → **Data and UI flow**. Add branches where the learner must choose an alternative and label the evidence for the choice.

choices.feedback: Feature decomposition is best understood as follows: Start with user-visible outcomes and convert them into explicit behavior, inputs, edge cases, and acceptance conditions. Break large capabilities into end-to-end slices that can be implemented and verified independently.

choices.feedback: This does not match the lesson's model. Reconsider the role of Forkify Application Planning.

choices.feedback: Engineering judgment balances clarity and cost. The concept should serve the requirement rather than become an end in itself.
choices.label: Use Forkify Application Planning when it makes intent, ownership, or data flow clearer; avoid adding it mechanically when a simpler, equally explicit solution is sufficient.

choices.feedback: This does not match the lesson's model. Reconsider the role of Forkify Application Planning.

researchSources.title: Project Overview and Planning (I)
researchSources.usage: Coverage research only; no transcript wording is canonical.

sections.body: Start with user-visible outcomes and convert them into explicit behavior, inputs, edge cases, and acceptance conditions. Break large capabilities into end-to-end slices that can be implemented and verified independently.
sections.title: Feature decomposition

sections.body: Architecture planning identifies data, responsibilities, boundaries, and communication paths. The goal is to reduce costly ambiguity about ownership and change, not predict every future file.
sections.title: Project architecture planning

sections.body: Describe where data originates, who owns it, how it changes, and how the UI observes those changes. Explicit flow prevents hidden state and scattered updates.
sections.title: Data and UI flow

summary: Forkify Application Planning connects Feature decomposition, Project architecture planning, Data and UI flow.
Use the topic to make behavior and decisions explicit rather than memorizing isolated syntax.
Verify understanding by explaining a new example and answering the topic quiz without looking back.
title: Forkify Application Planning

topicQuiz.failurePolicy: Show explanations, add missed objectives to local review, and allow immediate or later retry.

whyThisMatters: Forkify Application Planning is part of the mental model learners need before making reliable implementation decisions. It belongs to the **Application Architecture: Forkify** module.
